title: The One in Barbados (Part 2)

jokes.text: Do you have anything that will get us out of them?
jokes.setup: Ross excitedly offers paleontology conference passes to everyone
jokes.punchline: Chandler immediately asks for the opposite - a way to avoid them
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm with perfect timing and economy. Immediately deflates Ross's enthusiasm in character-specific way.

jokes.text: He's been in Minsk for eight years. If he gets too much direct sunlight...he'll die.
jokes.setup: Explaining why David would want to attend lectures instead of doing island activities
jokes.punchline: Escalates from reasonable explanation to absurd medical condition
jokes.explanation: Typical Phoebe logic - starts rational then veers into bizarre territory. Good escalation structure.

jokes.text: It's a blanket, Joe, not a cloak of invisibility.
jokes.setup: Joey talks about getting under a blanket with Charlie on the plane
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out the obvious flaw in Joey's plan
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor with good visual imagery. Chandler as voice of reason to Joey's obliviousness.

jokes.text: There are gonna be some pasty folks by the pool tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Looking at all the paleontologists at the conference
jokes.punchline: Joey observes they'll look pale and unhealthy in swimwear
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor playing on scientist stereotypes. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: That never happens.
jokes.setup: Joey says 'This happens all the time' about being recognized
jokes.punchline: Ross immediately contradicts him about his own recognition
jokes.explanation: Simple contradiction joke. Ross's slight jealousy/surprise at being recognized.

jokes.text: I dig you.
jokes.setup: Signing an autograph for a paleontology fan
jokes.punchline: Uses 'dig' - both slang and archaeological reference
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning that fits Ross's profession. Simple but well-integrated wordplay.

jokes.text: Dude, it's just Days of Our Lives. There's no 'the.'
jokes.setup: Joey incorrectly names his show as 'The Days of Our Lives'
jokes.punchline: Ross corrects him about his own show's title
jokes.explanation: Embarrassing moment for Joey - getting his own show's name wrong. Ross as unlikely corrector.

jokes.text: What's all your furniture pointed at?
jokes.setup: Woman says she doesn't own a TV
jokes.punchline: Joey asks the logical next question about furniture arrangement
jokes.explanation: One of Joey's best moments - perfectly logical question that reveals absurdity of TV-free life. Became widely quoted.

jokes.text: That's the same as, 'It has something to do with wind.'
jokes.setup: David gives complex scientific explanation of flight
jokes.punchline: Chandler reduces it to his original oversimplified version
jokes.explanation: Chandler deflating scientific pomposity with reductive logic. Good contrast between complex and simple.

jokes.text: Mike? Who's Mike?
jokes.setup: Phoebe mentions Mike casually in conversation
jokes.punchline: David doesn't know who her ex-boyfriend is
jokes.explanation: Awkward moment setting up relationship tension. More plot-serving than genuinely funny.

jokes.text: That's a blast from the past, huh?
jokes.setup: Phoebe just mentioned Mike and got caught
jokes.punchline: She tries to play it off as ancient history
jokes.explanation: Awkward recovery attempt. The discomfort is the comedy but it's not particularly clever.

jokes.text: She does?
jokes.setup: Monica supposedly calls Chandler 'Richard' all the time
jokes.punchline: Chandler's surprised reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat. Chandler's insecurity about Monica's ex showing through.

jokes.text: Well, at least you took me down with you.
jokes.setup: Phoebe makes the same mistake with Chandler/Richard names
jokes.punchline: Monica's dry observation about shared misery
jokes.explanation: Monica's practical approach to awkward situations. Good deadpan delivery implied.

jokes.text: You just did it again. Chandler. Your feelings for Chandler are certainly gone.
jokes.setup: Monica says her feelings for Richard are gone
jokes.punchline: Phoebe catches and corrects the same mistake again immediately
jokes.explanation: Quick callback that escalates the running gag. Shows the name confusion is a real problem.

jokes.text: Or Joey...for advice about pizza toppings or a burning sensation when you pee.
jokes.setup: Chandler lists who David should ask for advice
jokes.punchline: Categorizes Joey's expertise in oddly specific areas
jokes.explanation: Great character insight into Joey's areas of knowledge. Unexpected pairing of pizza and medical issues.

jokes.text: Oh, because his penis was too big.
jokes.setup: David asks why Phoebe and Mike broke up
jokes.punchline: Chandler gives completely inappropriate and absurd reason
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler inappropriate humor. Completely wrong context but perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. That's the kind of thing I do.
jokes.setup: Chandler just made an inappropriate joke
jokes.punchline: He acknowledges his own comedic pattern
jokes.explanation: Self-aware character moment. Chandler recognizing his own inappropriate humor reflex.

jokes.text: Well, we probably won't invite you to the wedding, but...
jokes.setup: David fantasizing about proposing and beating Mike
jokes.punchline: Adds petty detail about excluding Mike from wedding
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation of petty revenge fantasy. David's mild-mannered vindictiveness.

jokes.text: I'm pretending to read here.
jokes.setup: David continues asking for proposal advice
jokes.punchline: Chandler tries to avoid further involvement
jokes.explanation: Chandler's attempt to escape responsibility for his advice. Good economy and character.

jokes.text: You really should have been more clear about that.
jokes.setup: Joey clarifies he meant flip-flops, not underwear thong
jokes.punchline: Charlie points out the miscommunication was Joey's fault
jokes.explanation: Simple misunderstanding joke. Functional but predictable setup/payoff.

jokes.text: Yeah. She never misses these conferences.
jokes.setup: Joey guesses Britney Spears is at paleontology conference
jokes.punchline: Charlie plays along with the absurd suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response to Joey's randomness. Charlie showing dry wit.

jokes.text: You could say, 'Hey, Kenny, how come you're not Britney Spears?'
jokes.setup: Ross asks what to say to famous paleontologist
jokes.punchline: Joey suggests asking why he's not the pop star they just discussed
jokes.explanation: Joey's logic connecting unrelated topics. Good callback integration.

jokes.text: You got yourself a very weird deal.
jokes.setup: Both agree to wear thongs if the other does
jokes.punchline: Charlie acknowledges the strangeness of their agreement
jokes.explanation: Simple observation about mutual embarrassment pact. Mild but functional.

jokes.text: I wasn't gonna swim. I was gonna dig a hole.
jokes.setup: Joey says he'll go to the beach despite the rain
jokes.punchline: Reveals his beach activity doesn't require good weather
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Joey-like solution. Unexpected but logical in his childlike way.

jokes.text: because you are pink with barely controlled glee.
jokes.setup: Monica senses Chandler has news to share
jokes.punchline: Describes his physical state of excitement
jokes.explanation: Vivid description of Chandler's excitement. 'Barely controlled glee' is excellent phrasing.

jokes.text: I did! A penis one!
jokes.setup: Monica asks if he made an inappropriate joke instead of advice
jokes.punchline: Chandler proudly confirms he did both
jokes.explanation: Chandler's pride in his inappropriate humor. Good callback structure.

jokes.text: It rains all day long and nobody watches TV, and Ross is famous.
jokes.setup: Joey explains how everything is wrong in Barbados
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly bizarre reversals of normal order
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from weather complaint to existential crisis. Ross being famous as ultimate bizarro-world sign.

jokes.text: Monica's hair is twice as big as it was when we landed.
jokes.setup: Joey continues listing what's wrong with Barbados
jokes.punchline: Points out Monica's humidity-induced hair expansion
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about humidity effects. Sets up running hair joke for episode.

jokes.text: That's why our honeymoon photos look like me and Diana Ross.
jokes.setup: Monica explains her hair gets bigger in humidity
jokes.punchline: Compares honeymoon photos to herself with famous big-haired singer
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual comparison. Diana Ross reference perfectly captures the hair situation with economy and humor.

jokes.text: Not Joey. No.
jokes.setup: Joey says he wishes he had someone to share romantic Barbados with
jokes.punchline: Rachel and Phoebe simultaneously reject the idea
jokes.explanation: Quick rejection beat. Simple but effective group dynamics.

jokes.text: I was just lusting after Chandler.
jokes.setup: After rejecting Joey as romantic companion
jokes.punchline: Phoebe claims she was thinking about Chandler instead
jokes.explanation: Unexpected misdirection. Phoebe's casual admission of inappropriate thoughts.

jokes.text: That's one-seventieth of a carat. And the clarity is quite poor.
jokes.setup: David shows his tiny engagement ring
jokes.punchline: He honestly describes its terrible quality in technical terms
jokes.explanation: David's scientific honesty about his pathetic ring. Self-deprecating humor with technical precision.

jokes.text: Are you leaving the Supremes?
jokes.setup: Monica wants to talk to Phoebe privately
jokes.punchline: Phoebe references Monica's Diana Ross hair
jokes.explanation: Good callback extending the hair joke. Supremes reference adds musical group context.

jokes.text: You know? It's every girl's dream.
jokes.setup: Describing forcing Mike to marry her against his will
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically calls it romantic
jokes.explanation: Sarcastic take on forced romance. Phoebe acknowledging the absurdity.

jokes.text: Don't look at me that way, Roseanne Roseannadanna.
jokes.setup: Phoebe keeps saying Mike instead of David
jokes.punchline: References Monica's big hair with SNL character name
jokes.explanation: Another hair reference with classic SNL character. Good cultural callback.

jokes.text: The chocolates aren't here. Damn it!
jokes.setup: Joey keeps checking for pillow chocolates
jokes.punchline: His continued disappointment
jokes.explanation: Running gag about Joey's priorities. Simple but character-consistent.

jokes.text: Secret teapot.
jokes.setup: Asked what he was looking for online
jokes.punchline: Mysterious and unexplained reference
jokes.explanation: Completely random and unexplained Joey moment. The mystery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: 'Nude Pictures of Anna Kournikova.'
jokes.setup: Explaining what email subject line he clicked
jokes.punchline: Reveals he fell for obvious spam
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey gullibility. Predictable but fits character perfectly.

jokes.text: She's never even won a major tournament.
jokes.setup: Discussing Anna Kournikova nude photos
jokes.punchline: Charlie focuses on tennis achievements instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie response - academic focus on sports stats rather than scandal. Great character moment.

jokes.text: Well, I tried Billie Jean King, but...
jokes.setup: Charlie mentions tennis achievements
jokes.punchline: Joey implies he looked for nude photos of elderly tennis legend
jokes.explanation: Escalates absurdity by suggesting even less appropriate target. Joey's logic is perfectly wrong.

jokes.text: You know, you and Monica have the same I'm-gonna-kill-you look.
jokes.setup: Ross is angry about his deleted speech
jokes.punchline: Joey compares his expression to Monica's anger face
jokes.explanation: Good sibling observation. Joey recognizing family anger patterns.

jokes.text: I don't think your monologue from Star Wars is gonna help me right now.
jokes.setup: Joey offers a memorized speech for auditions
jokes.punchline: Ross correctly guesses it's inappropriate sci-fi material
jokes.explanation: Ross immediately understanding Joey's unhelpful suggestion. Good contrast between their worlds.

jokes.text: Ooh, ooh. Any chance any of this happened in a galaxy far, far away?
jokes.setup: Ross explaining paleontology concepts
jokes.punchline: Joey tries to connect it to Star Wars
jokes.explanation: Joey trying to make science relatable through pop culture. Good callback integration.

jokes.text: I can't hear through all this damned hair!
jokes.setup: Monica talking on phone with big hair
jokes.punchline: Claims hair is interfering with phone call
jokes.explanation: Escalates the hair gag to absurd practical problems. Good escalation of running visual gag.

jokes.text: And while I've got you, you've got curly hair. What do you do in the humidity?
jokes.setup: Monica calling Mike about Phoebe
jokes.punchline: She can't help asking for hair advice during crisis call
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica moment - even in crisis, she seeks practical solutions. Hair obsession overcomes social norms.

jokes.text: Aw, I hope you're happy too, honey.
jokes.setup: Monica says 'I hope you're happy' accusingly
jokes.punchline: Chandler takes it as genuine well-wish
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler missing sarcasm. Perfect character moment of literal interpretation.

jokes.text: Meddler! Meddler!
jokes.setup: Monica blaming Chandler for meddling
jokes.punchline: He throws the accusation back repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Simple repetitive comeback. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: This vacation sucks!
jokes.setup: Complex relationship drama they've created
jokes.punchline: Chandler reduces it to vacation complaint
jokes.explanation: Good understatement reducing complex drama to simple vacation rating.

jokes.text: And that's the most sex I'm gonna have this weekend.
jokes.setup: Name tag application process with attractive woman
jokes.punchline: Joey acknowledges this is his peak romantic moment
jokes.explanation: Self-aware Joey moment acknowledging his poor romantic prospects. Good self-deprecating humor.

jokes.text: Well, in that case, should I make sure it's on real good?
jokes.setup: Joey saying this is his only sexual contact
jokes.punchline: Woman offers to make it more thorough
jokes.explanation: Escalates Joey's self-deprecating moment. Woman playing along with the joke.

jokes.text: Actually, I did it, Ross. You remembered shockingly little of your own speech.
jokes.setup: Ross thanks everyone for recreating his speech
jokes.punchline: Charlie points out Ross barely contributed
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Ross's gratitude. Charlie being academically precise about contributions.

jokes.text: Yes, but I did make a pyramid out of the bath products.
jokes.setup: Charlie pointing out he didn't help much
jokes.punchline: Ross defends with completely unrelated achievement
jokes.explanation: Ross's defensive pride in irrelevant accomplishment. Good non-sequitur that fits his character.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, I love you.
jokes.setup: Rachel suggests putting water in champagne bottle to avoid charges
jokes.punchline: Ross is overwhelmed by her frugality
jokes.explanation: Ross's overreaction to Rachel's cheap trick. Sets up their growing connection.

jokes.text: Hey! High-five!
jokes.setup: Both Ross and Charlie had gay former partners
jokes.punchline: Charlie suggests celebrating this awkward commonality
jokes.explanation: Awkward attempt to bond over relationship failures. Cringe humor from inappropriate celebration.

jokes.text: 'Hi. Just dropped by to say your wife's gay.'
jokes.setup: Discussing why people don't warn about obvious signs
jokes.punchline: Ross imagines absurdly direct warning note
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward imaginary scenario. Good economy and absurdist logic.

jokes.text: Me neither.
jokes.setup: Ross says he didn't suggest threesome after divorce
jokes.punchline: Charlie quickly agrees she didn't either
jokes.explanation: Awkward beat suggesting maybe she did consider it. The quickness implies guilt.

jokes.text: Not enough pills in the world, Rach.
jokes.setup: Rachel mentions old lady wanted to take Joey home
jokes.punchline: Joey emphatically rejects the idea
jokes.explanation: Good visual hyperbole. Joey's emphatic rejection with pharmaceutical context.

jokes.text: and I'm sunburned. From, you know, the rain.
jokes.setup: Rachel denies blushing, claims sunburn
jokes.punchline: Attributes sunburn to the rainy weather
jokes.explanation: Absurd excuse that acknowledges the weather problem. Good callback integration.

jokes.text: You know, it's very hard to take you seriously when you look like that.
jokes.setup: Monica making serious point about relationships
jokes.punchline: Chandler references her ridiculous hair
jokes.explanation: Undermines serious moment with visual callback. Good tension break.

jokes.text: 'Uh, Phoebe, um, I would be honored if, uh, uh...' Spit it out, David!
jokes.setup: Monica observing David's slow speaking pattern
jokes.punchline: She mimics his halting speech and gets impatient
jokes.explanation: Monica's impatience with David's verbose speaking style. Good character observation.

jokes.text: It's David, actually.
jokes.setup: Phoebe says 'Oh my God, Mike' when she sees him
jokes.punchline: David politely corrects her mid-proposal
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to having his proposal interrupted by ex-boyfriend confusion. Great timing and character.

jokes.text: It's the humidity!
jokes.setup: Mike sees Monica's huge hair
jokes.punchline: Monica immediately explains the obvious
jokes.explanation: Monica's automatic defense of her appearance. Good callback continuation.

jokes.text: Well, that's fair, you've had a long trip.
jokes.setup: Mike takes David's chair during the proposal
jokes.punchline: David politely accommodates his romantic rival
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly polite response to being interrupted during proposal. Perfect David character moment - overly accommodating even in crisis.

jokes.text: Kind of steps on the toes of what I was going to say.
jokes.setup: Mike just declared his love and intent to propose
jokes.punchline: David understates the devastating impact on his own proposal
jokes.explanation: Masterful understatement of romantic devastation. David's scientific precision applied to heartbreak.

jokes.text: All right, but after this, I want to see you outside. If the rain stops.
jokes.setup: David challenging Mike to a fight
jokes.punchline: Adds weather condition to his threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from anger to practical concerns. David can't even issue a threat without weather considerations. Brilliant character comedy.

jokes.text: I wouldn't brag about that, big guy.
jokes.setup: David mentions he has an engagement ring
jokes.punchline: Mike dismisses the tiny ring as unimpressive
jokes.explanation: Callback to David's pathetic ring. Simple but effective put-down.

jokes.text: Ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Phoebe says no to Mike's proposal
jokes.punchline: David's brief moment of triumph
jokes.explanation: Brief victory moment before the real answer. Simple but effective beat.

jokes.text: It was a good trip.
jokes.setup: David listing terrible things that happened to him in Minsk
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically summarizes the awful experience
jokes.explanation: David's dry acceptance of his string of bad luck. Perfect understatement after listing disasters.

jokes.text: Because of our meddling! All right?
jokes.setup: Mike and Phoebe getting back together
jokes.punchline: Monica takes credit for the happy ending through their interference
jokes.explanation: Monica claiming victory for their disastrous interference. Good callback resolution.

jokes.text: Hey! Remember when I had corneas?
jokes.setup: Everyone excited about the sun coming out
jokes.punchline: Chandler references eye damage from Monica's hair
jokes.explanation: Escalates hair gag to physical injury. Excellent escalation and quotable line.

jokes.text: She's all leathery and wrinkled. I'm so jealous!
jokes.setup: Monica pointing out sun-damaged woman by pool
jokes.punchline: Expresses envy for the obvious skin damage
jokes.explanation: Monica's desperate desire for sun exposure. Ironic envy of obvious damage.

jokes.text: Not to mention Coldsaurus.
jokes.setup: Ross listing newly discovered dinosaur species
jokes.punchline: Joey makes up cold-related dinosaur name
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay fitting Joey's pattern of made-up terms. Weather-appropriate dinosaur.

jokes.text: You're kidding, right?
jokes.setup: Joey laughs at 'Homo erectus' in speech
jokes.punchline: Ross can't believe Joey finds scientific term funny
jokes.explanation: Ross's disbelief at Joey's immaturity. Simple reaction to juvenile humor.

jokes.text: Okay, now we're just holding hands.
jokes.setup: Overly enthusiastic fan shaking Ross's hand too long
jokes.punchline: Ross points out the inappropriate duration
jokes.explanation: Awkward physical boundary crossing. Good cringe humor from fan interaction.

jokes.text: Hey, I better not find you naked in my hotel room.
jokes.setup: Charlie calling herself Ross's groupie
jokes.punchline: Ross references stereotypical groupie behavior
jokes.explanation: Standard groupie reference escalating the rock star comparison. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Look, I took it too far.
jokes.setup: Ross made suggestive groupie comment
jokes.punchline: Immediately acknowledges he crossed a line
jokes.explanation: Ross's self-awareness about inappropriate comment. Quick recovery beat.

jokes.text: You've got it going on.
jokes.setup: Monica insults paleontologists' physiques then realizes she's talking to some
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt to exclude them from the insult
jokes.explanation: Monica's awkward recovery from accidental insult. Weak save attempt.

jokes.text: And while I say it's cute, others disagree, and I'm lying.
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining Monica's competitiveness is cute
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself and admits he was lying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler honesty spiral. Great escalation from diplomatic to brutally honest in one sentence.

jokes.text: I punched you. And? Phoebe. And? I clunked your heads together.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking what happened during their last ping pong game
jokes.punchline: Monica's escalating violence admission
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Monica's competitive violence. Each revelation gets worse.

jokes.text: I don't know what happens if the sea turtle catches you.
jokes.setup: Joey wants someone to go snorkeling with sea turtle
jokes.punchline: Admits fear of unknown turtle consequences
jokes.explanation: Joey's childlike fear of harmless sea creature. Good absurdist logic.

jokes.text: So we're just four losers. Super.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to play ping pong without keeping score
jokes.punchline: Monica's sarcastic take on non-competitive games
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica response to non-competitive activity. Character-driven sarcasm.

jokes.text: This scar? That's from Pictionary.
jokes.setup: Warning about Monica's competitiveness
jokes.punchline: Claims physical injury from board game
jokes.explanation: Escalates Monica's competitiveness to absurd injury level. Perfectly unexpected and quotable.

jokes.text: Oh, dear God, there's two of them.
jokes.setup: Mike reveals he's also competitive at ping pong
jokes.punchline: Chandler realizes they have two ultra-competitive players
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to discovering matched competitive opponents. Sets up intense game.

jokes.text: Honey, try to focus the trash talk on him.
jokes.setup: Mike calls them 'girls' in trash talk
jokes.punchline: Chandler asks to be excluded from gender-based insults
jokes.explanation: Chandler's reasonable request during competitive trash talk. Good husband support moment.

jokes.text: Weather bitch.
jokes.setup: Weather report about beautiful weather everywhere else
jokes.punchline: Monica's anger at the weather reporter
jokes.explanation: Monica's irrational anger at messenger. Good escalation of weather frustration.

jokes.text: Well, hey!
jokes.setup: Joey says smart people are dull
jokes.punchline: Ross takes mild offense
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat. Ross mildly defending smart people.

jokes.text: George! Who? Curious George!
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to say she's curious
jokes.punchline: Makes pop culture reference that doesn't quite work
jokes.explanation: Rachel's awkward attempt at clever wordplay. The struggle makes it funnier.

jokes.text: He had a paper route.
jokes.setup: Discussing Curious George
jokes.punchline: Rachel adds completely wrong detail about the character
jokes.explanation: Completely wrong detail about children's character. Rachel's confident wrongness.

jokes.text: This is the first time in our marriage that I've felt like the more attractive one.
jokes.setup: Monica looking disheveled and competitive
jokes.punchline: Chandler admits he usually feels less attractive
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-deprecating humor revealing relationship dynamic. Honest and funny.

jokes.text: Knock that dog off her head.
jokes.setup: Monica's disheveled hair during ping pong
jokes.punchline: Mike describes her hair as a dog
jokes.explanation: Escalates hair joke to animal comparison. Good visual callback.

jokes.text: I sleep with him!
jokes.setup: Mike making fun of her appearance
jokes.punchline: Monica's defensive comeback about relationship status
jokes.explanation: Monica's competitive comeback using relationship status as weapon.

jokes.text: You should look in the mirror before you call yourself that.
jokes.setup: Mike asks how it felt losing to a girl
jokes.punchline: Monica insults his appearance as response
jokes.explanation: Standard competitive insult. Functional trash talk but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Should I use my invisibility to fight crime or for evil?
jokes.setup: Overwhelmed by intense ping pong competition
jokes.punchline: Chandler fantasizes about escaping reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect escape fantasy from domestic chaos. Chandler's absurdist coping mechanism.

jokes.text: 'Serve the ball, chump.' Okay, better comebacks, Mike. Better comebacks.
jokes.setup: Monica calls him 'chump'
jokes.punchline: Mike critiques his own trash talk performance
jokes.explanation: Self-aware competitive moment. Mike acknowledging his weak trash talk.

jokes.text: Well, get out!
jokes.setup: Ross introducing his wife Nancy after Charlie met married man
jokes.punchline: Charlie's surprised/embarrassed reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple surprise reaction to awkward situation. Functional but basic.

jokes.text: I knew that was him!
jokes.setup: Charlie mentions Joey laughing during speech
jokes.punchline: Ross identifies Joey as the disruptive audience member
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to earlier disruption. Ross confirming his suspicions.

jokes.text: Go! Go! Go!
jokes.setup: Paleontologists trying to throw Ross in pool
jokes.punchline: Ross and Charlie running away
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy chase sequence. Simple slapstick but functional.

jokes.text: Your hand is blistered, you can barely stand, your hair is inexplicable!
jokes.setup: Monica's deteriorating physical state from ping pong
jokes.punchline: Chandler lists her increasingly absurd condition
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation culminating in hair observation. 'Inexplicable' is excellent word choice.

jokes.text: Well, this is my sickness!
jokes.setup: Chandler references marriage vows about sickness and health
jokes.punchline: Monica categorizes competitiveness as the sickness he must accept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant application of marriage vows to competitive obsession. Perfect Monica character moment.

jokes.text: Okay, we're taking that paddle home, mister.
jokes.setup: Chandler successfully playing ping pong
jokes.punchline: Monica's possessive reaction to his skill
jokes.explanation: Monica claiming ownership of Chandler's unexpected skill. Good possessive reaction.

jokes.text: But you suck.
jokes.setup: Monica giving loving speech about supporting Chandler
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts it with brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect undercut of romantic moment with characteristic Monica honesty. Great economy and timing.

jokes.text: It's like watching porn.
jokes.setup: Watching skilled ping pong match
jokes.punchline: Monica's inappropriate comparison
jokes.explanation: Monica's inappropriate excitement about athletic skill. Good escalation of enthusiasm.

jokes.text: When did you stop sucking?
jokes.setup: Discovering Chandler is good at ping pong
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt question about his improvement
jokes.explanation: Monica's direct questioning style. Good callback to established incompetence.

jokes.text: That's why.
jokes.setup: Monica excited about entering doubles tournaments
jokes.punchline: Chandler reveals he hid his skill to avoid this reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect reveal of Chandler's strategic incompetence. Brilliant character moment showing his understanding of Monica.

jokes.text: You know, we, as a group, are not the coolest.
jokes.setup: Paleontologists split into hunting parties named after dinosaur types
jokes.punchline: Ross acknowledges the inherent nerdiness
jokes.explanation: Ross's rare moment of self-awareness about paleontology nerdiness. Good understated delivery.

tmdb_overview: The Barbados trip concludes with surprise romances, rekindled feelings, and unexpected confessions that change the dynamic of the group.
